title: pilosella bauhini subsp. rodnensis
intro: Pilosella bauhini subsp. rodnensis, also known as Rodna puszcza woolly meadow grass or simply puszcza, is a rare subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating digestive issues and inflammatory conditions, due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Pilosella bauhini subsp. rodnensis include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as a natural antiseptic. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures, infusions, and decoctions, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
